title: Caching
description: Response caching and the memory, Redis, Deno KV and tiered adapters

blocks.text: Cache responses per route with caching.ttl (seconds) in defineApi. Only 2xx (non-204) responses are cached; a hit replays the original status.

On role-protected routes, cache keys are namespaced per user via getRateLimitIdentifier. If that hook is unset, response caching is skipped on those routes — a shared key would leak one user's response to another. Public routes cache by URL.
Set the adapter once via the cache field. All live in @hushkey/howl/api/cache and share the CacheAdapter interface, so they're interchangeable.

rows: Redis-backed, shared across instances. Accepts an ioredis-like client.
Deno KV — shared on Deploy, per-process locally.
Tiered: primary with a timeout, falling back to a secondary.

blocks.text: Response caching is idempotent, so a per-instance memory cache is fine. Counters must be shared. Use a tiered cache for responses and a shared backend for counters.
tryCache races the primary against a timeout (default 300ms) and falls back to the secondary if it is slow or errors — so a Redis hiccup degrades to local memory instead of failing the request.
Any object implementing CacheAdapter plugs in. Implement get/set/delete; add an atomic incr if you want the adapter usable as a shared rateLimitCache.
